tonight.emptyTitle: No bedtime to suggest tonight yet.
tonight.emptyBody: Log a few nights and this will suggest a bedtime that fits your rhythm — or pick a wake time in the calculator below.
tonight.supportConflict: Tomorrow starts earlier than your body clock (usually around {bedtime}). This is as early as is healthy to shift in one night; move ~30 min earlier each night to fully adjust over ~{nights, plural, one {# night} other {# nights}}.
tonight.supportNoCommitments: No timed commitments tomorrow — this keeps your usual rhythm.
tonight.tooLateFits: going to bed now still fits about {count, plural, one {# cycle} other {# cycles}}.
tonight.tooLateLessThanOne: less than one full cycle fits before your wake time.

typicalNight.label: Your typical night
typicalNight.srWithData: Across {count, plural, one {# night} other {# nights}} with data{avg}.
typicalNight.srNoData: No nights with sleep data in this period yet.
typicalNight.avgPerNightHint: {count, plural, one {# night} other {# nights}} with data

rhythm.label: Sleep rhythm
rhythm.srNoData: No nights with times in this period yet.
rhythm.srSummary: Bedtime to wake across {count, plural, one {# night} other {# nights}}.{usual}
rhythm.empty: Once a night or two has bedtimes and wake times, your rhythm shows here — bed to wake, night over night.
rhythm.legend: Each row is a night, bed to wake. Solid: logged · hatched: derived from your calendar{band}.

history.label: Morning check-ins
history.srAvgQuality: Average sleep quality {value} of 4 across {count, plural, one {# morning} other {# mornings}}
history.srAvgSleepiness: average tiredness {value} of 4 across {count, plural, one {# morning} other {# mornings}}
history.chartAriaLabel: Sleep quality and morning tiredness per night
history.legend: Solid line: quality (higher is better) · dashed: tiredness (lower is better)
history.empty: Rate how you slept in the morning check-in and your quality and sleepiness trends will show here, night over night.

hints.label: Hints · last {days} days
hints.unlock: Log {count, plural, one {# more morning} other {# more mornings}} to unlock sleep hints — they compare how you score after different kinds of nights, against your <link>sleep settings</link>.
hints.noPatterns: No patterns stand out yet — your scores don’t differ much across night lengths or bedtimes in the last {days} days.
hints.durationShortBody: Across {count, plural, one {# logged morning} other {# logged mornings}}, nights under your {target} target scored noticeably worse than full ones.
hints.durationLongBody: Across {count, plural, one {# logged morning} other {# logged mornings}}, nights at or over your {target} target scored worse than shorter ones — the target may not fit.
hints.regularityBody: Your bedtime varies by about ±{spread} minutes across {count, plural, one {# logged morning} other {# logged mornings}} — a steadier bedtime usually beats extra time in bed.
hints.cycleAlignedBody: Across {count, plural, one {# logged morning} other {# logged mornings}}, waking near a whole number of {cycle}-minute cycles scored better — the calculator’s bedtimes should help.
hints.cycleNeutralTitle: Cycle endings don’t seem to matter
hints.cycleNeutralBody: Across {count, plural, one {# logged morning} other {# logged mornings}}, cycle-aligned nights didn’t score better — duration and regularity matter more for you.

calculator.title: Cycle calculator
calculator.rowDetail: {duration} asleep · ≈ {cycles, plural, one {# cycle} other {# cycles}}
calculator.description: Sleep-cycle length varies night to night, so these are estimates — not exact wake moments. Uses your {cycle}-minute cycles and {onset} minutes to fall asleep — <link>adjust in Settings</link>.

checkin.title: Good morning — how did you sleep?
checkin.subtitle: A few seconds now sharpens your sleep hints. Everything is optional and stays private to you.
checkin.dismiss: Dismiss the morning check-in for today

fields.qualityDescription: Tap a selected level again to clear.
fields.fatigueDescription: Tap a selected level again to clear.

logNight.title: Log a night
logNight.description: Backfill or correct a morning — saving replaces anything already logged for that date.
logNight.morningOfDescription: The date you woke up. Future dates can’t be logged.
logNight.confirmBody: This removes your check-in for {date} — quality, fatigue, times and note. Nights derived from your calendar are not affected.
